text: Safer use of medicines and vaccines for everyone everywhere Uppsala Monitoring Centre (UMC) – advancing the science of pharmacovigilance and transforming its practice through technological innovation Solutions and services to support ​national pharmacovigilance systems at all levels of maturity Terminology and standards for efficiency and interoperability in the pharmaceutical industry UMC helps strengthen medicines and vaccine safety systems worldwide.

We offer specialised products, services, and expertise to support regulators and national authorities throughout the full range of their pharmacovigilance efforts, from data capture to management, analysis, and sharing. UMC supports the World Health Organization, helping to build capacity, efficiency, and effectiveness by providing tools and training that streamline pharmacovigilance workflows.

The WHO Programme for International Drug Monitoring Standardising terminology and data formats Standardisation is key to providing certainty and interoperability through the full lifecycle of medicine and vaccine safety information. Standardised terminology and data exchange formats make effective global pharmacovigilance possible.

UMC embraces standardisation in all our pharmacovigilance products and supports development of global standards. UMC is a leading centre for pharmacovigilance and data researchers, working to advance the science of pharmacovigilance and transform its practice for the better.

Our long-term commitment to scientific development, innovation, and collaboration drive advances in areas such as data-driven discovery, artificial intelligence, and the expansion of data sources available to safety surveillance work.

Celebrating two decades of pharmacovigilance collaboration in the Americas – New UR article From mandate to practice: How Butantan embedded UMC’s drug dictionary into Brazil's pharmacovigilance system – New UR article Technovigilance keeps hearts at Instituto Nacional de Cardiología Ignacio Chávez beating – New UR article How to perform better disproportionality analyses – New podcast episode.
